meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: Project Management for Students: Stop Getting Stuck on Apps
content.seo_description: Break through the intermediate plateau. Learn project management strategies for AI builders using Antigravity and Claude Code to finish your first app.
content.answer_block: You are stuck because you are coding without a roadmap. To break through, adopt a modular project management framework that treats your app as a series of small, shippable milestones. Use Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code to automate implementation, ensuring you maintain momentum from prototype to production.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks in tutorial hell, copying code that works in a sandbox but crumbles the moment you try to build something original. The excitement of your first AI project has curdled into the frustration of endless debugging, broken dependencies, and a feature list that seems to grow faster than your ability to write code. It’s not a lack of talent; it’s a lack of process.

Real-world engineering isn't about writing the perfect line of code—it’s about managing complexity. By treating your development lifecycle with the rigor of a professional architect, you stop guessing and start building. This guide will show you how to leverage AI-native tooling to turn that overwhelming roadmap into a series of decisive, completed victories.

steps.title: Define the Minimum Viable Scope
steps.description: Stop building 'platforms' and start building 'features'. Identify the single core value proposition of your app.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project idea and break it down into the smallest possible functional MVP. Output a list of 5 essential features, ignoring all nice-to-haves.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --task 'Create a project roadmap document outlining the 5 MVP features defined in Antigravity.'

steps.title: Architect the Data Flow
steps.description: Map how data moves from user input to AI processing and back, preventing spaghetti code before it starts.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a high-level sequence diagram for a data-driven AI application. Define the state management requirements for the frontend.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --task 'Generate the folder structure and skeleton files for the proposed architecture.'

steps.title: Build with Modular Components
steps.description: Isolate your AI logic from your UI to make debugging effortless.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this feature into a decoupled service-based architecture where the AI logic is an independent module.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --task 'Implement the service module and write unit tests for the core logic.'

steps.title: Automate Iterative Feedback
steps.description: Use Claude Code to run continuous integration checks on your local environment to catch bugs early.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a suite of integration tests that validate the AI response format against my schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --task 'Run the integration tests and fix any failing dependencies automatically.'

steps.title: Ship and Document
steps.description: Finalize your build and generate professional documentation to showcase your work for your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Based on the code I've written, generate a README.md that explains the architecture, the tech stack, and the problem solved.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --task 'Finalize the project documentation and prepare the codebase for deployment.'

pro_tips: Always commit your code before asking Claude Code to refactor to ensure you can revert if the logic diverges.
Treat your AI prompts like API contracts; if the input schema is unstable, your app will never be production-ready.
Use a 'scratchpad' file for experimental code to avoid cluttering your main production modules.
Prioritize error handling early; an AI that fails silently is 10x harder to debug than one that throws descriptive exceptions.
Automate your environment setup; if you can't spin up a fresh dev environment in under 5 minutes, you have too much technical debt.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, focus on gathering user feedback and implementing a CI/CD pipeline to automate your deployment process.
content.conversion_block: You have the roadmap, the tools, and the professional mindset to stop being a student and start being a developer. The only thing missing is your first commit. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
